question_text: A light ray travels from air into a glass block at an angle of 30° to the normal. The light slows down in the glass. Which statement best explains what happens to the light ray?

options.label: A
options.text: The light ray bends away from the normal because it is slowing down

options.label: B
options.text: The light ray bends towards the normal because it is slowing down

options.label: C
options.text: The light ray continues in a straight line because the angle is less than 45°

options.label: D
options.text: The light ray bends away from the normal because glass is denser than air

correct_answer: The light ray bends towards the normal because it is slowing down
explanation: When light travels from a less dense medium (air) into a denser medium (glass), it slows down. This change in speed causes the light to refract (bend) towards the normal. The normal is an imaginary line perpendicular to the surface. Remember: denser medium = slower speed = bends towards the normal.
